input.background: An early publication from the Framingham Heart Study identified 51 incidences of coronary disease among 424 men with high serum cholesterol (245 mg per 100 mL and above) over a 6-year period. Among 454 men with lower cholesterol (less than 210 mg per 100 mL), there were 16 incident cases.
input.question: Determine the 95% confidence interval for the estimated relative risk (RR).

output.answer: The $95 \%$ confidence interval for the $\widehat{R R}$ is 1.98 to 5.89.

serum_cholesterol_high.description: Number of coronary disease cases and total number of men with high serum cholesterol (245 mg per 100 mL and above).

serum_cholesterol_low.description: Number of coronary disease cases and total number of men with lower serum cholesterol (less than 210 mg per 100 mL).

relative_risk.description: The estimated relative risk (RR) of coronary disease between men with high serum cholesterol and men with lower serum cholesterol.

confidence_interval.description: The confidence level (95%) for the interval estimation of the relative risk.
